content:
# Revelation Chapter 3 — Disagreement Log
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **Book:** Revelation | **Chapter:** 3

**Verse 1 — "dead you are" vs. "you are dead":**
- EMET: "dead you are" (preserving Greek predicate-first order)
- LOGOS: "and yet you are dead"
- PNEUMA: "but you are dead"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "and yet you are dead" following LOGOS. The adversative captures the ironic contrast between reputation and reality.

**Verse 2 — "Become watching" vs. "Be watchful" vs. "Wake up!":**
- EMET: "Become watching"
- LOGOS: "Be watchful"
- PNEUMA: "Wake up!"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Be watchful" following LOGOS. PNEUMA's "Wake up!" is dynamic but loses the ongoing watchfulness of the present imperative.

**Verse 2 — "the remaining things which were about to die" vs. "what remains before it dies completely":**
- EMET: "the remaining [things] which were about to die"
- LOGOS: "the things that remain, which were about to die"
- PNEUMA: "what remains before it dies completely"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS's rendering. PNEUMA's "completely" is not in the Greek.

**Verse 2 — "having been fulfilled" vs. "fulfilled" vs. "complete":**
- EMET: "having been fulfilled"
- LOGOS: "fulfilled"
- PNEUMA: "complete"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "fulfilled" following LOGOS.

**Verse 3 — "you will not know" vs. "you shall by no means know" vs. "you will have no idea":**
- EMET: "you will not know"
- LOGOS: "you shall by no means know"
- PNEUMA: "you will have no idea"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS's rendering to capture the emphatic OU ME double negative.

**Verse 4 — "names" vs. "people":**
- EMET: "names"
- LOGOS: "names"
- PNEUMA: "people"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "names" (2-1 majority), preserving the Semitism and name/identity theme.

**Verse 4 — "defile/stain":**
- EMET: "defile"
- LOGOS: "defiled"
- PNEUMA: "stained"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "defiled" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 5 — "scroll of the life" vs. "book of life":**
- EMET: "scroll of the life"
- LOGOS: "book of life"
- PNEUMA: "book of life"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "book of life" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 5 — "confess/acknowledge":**
- EMET: "confess"
- LOGOS: "confess"
- PNEUMA: "acknowledge"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "confess" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 7 — "the key of the David" vs. "the key of David":**
- EMET: "the key of the David"
- LOGOS: "the key of David"
- PNEUMA: "the key of David"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "the key of David" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 8 — Syntactic handling of anacoluthon:**
- EMET: preserves broken syntax with dashes
- LOGOS: smooths to readable English with "Behold"
- PNEUMA: smooths with "Look"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS's rendering for comprehension while retaining formal weight.

**Verse 8 — "a little power/little strength":**
- EMET: "a little power"
- LOGOS: "a little power"
- PNEUMA: "little strength"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "a little power" (2-1 majority). DYNAMIS is "power" not "strength."

**Verse 9 — "I give from" vs. "I will make those of" vs. "I will make those from":**
- EMET: "I give from the synagogue of the Satanas"
- LOGOS: "I give those of the synagogue of Satan"
- PNEUMA: "I will make those from the synagogue of Satan"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted a hybrid following LOGOS's structure with future tense clarity.

**Verse 9 — "bow down/worship/fall at your feet":**
- EMET: "bow down before the feet of you"
- LOGOS: "worship before your feet"
- PNEUMA: "fall at your feet"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "worship before your feet" following LOGOS. PROSKYNEO carries full worship connotation.

**Verse 10 — "the word of the endurance of me" vs. "the word of my patient endurance" vs. "my command to endure":**
- EMET: "the word of the endurance of me"
- LOGOS: "the word of my patient endurance"
- PNEUMA: "my command to endure"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS's rendering. PNEUMA's "command to endure" interprets the genitive too narrowly.

**Verse 10 — "the whole inhabited earth" vs. "the whole world":**
- EMET: "the whole inhabited [earth]"
- LOGOS: "the whole inhabited earth"
- PNEUMA: "the whole world"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "the whole inhabited earth" (2-1 majority). OIKOUMENE specifically means the inhabited world.

**Verse 11 — "swiftly/quickly/soon":**
- EMET: "swiftly"
- LOGOS: "quickly"
- PNEUMA: "soon"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "quickly" following LOGOS, consistent with 2:16.

**Verse 12 — "the new Ierousalem" vs. "the new Jerusalem":**
- EMET: "the new Ierousalem"
- LOGOS: "the new Jerusalem"
- PNEUMA: "the new Jerusalem"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "the new Jerusalem" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 12 — "the name of me, the new" vs. "my own new name":**
- EMET: "the name of me, the new"
- LOGOS: "my own new name"
- PNEUMA: "my own new name"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "my own new name" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 14 — "the beginning of the creation of God" vs. "the origin of all God's creation":**
- EMET: "the beginning of the creation of the God"
- LOGOS: "the beginning of the creation of God"
- PNEUMA: "the origin of all God's creation"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS. PNEUMA's "origin" is valid for ARCHE but "beginning" preserves the semantic range. PNEUMA's "all" is not in the Greek.

**Verse 15 — "Would that" vs. "I wish":**
- EMET: "Would that cold you were or hot"
- LOGOS: "Would that you were cold or hot!"
- PNEUMA: "I wish you were either cold or hot!"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS's rendering.

**Verse 16 — "vomit/spit out":**
- EMET: "vomit"
- LOGOS: "vomit"
- PNEUMA: "spit out"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "vomit" (2-1 majority). EMESAI means to vomit; "spit out" softens the intended shock.

**Verse 17 — "I have become wealthy" vs. "I have prospered" vs. "I have acquired wealth":**
- EMET: "I have become wealthy"
- LOGOS: "I have prospered"
- PNEUMA: "I have acquired wealth"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "I have prospered" following LOGOS.

**Verse 17 — Five adjectives phrasing:**
- EMET: "the wretched and pitiable and poor and blind and naked"
- LOGOS: "the wretched one and pitiable and poor and blind and naked"
- PNEUMA: "wretched, pitiful, poor, blind, and naked"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS. The article and polysyndeton preserve the Greek's intensifying effect.

**Verse 18 — "gold having been fired from fire" vs. "gold refined by fire":**
- EMET: "gold having been fired from fire"
- LOGOS: "gold refined by fire"
- PNEUMA: "gold refined by fire"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "gold refined by fire" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 18 — "eye-salve/eye salve/ointment":**
- EMET: "eye-salve"
- LOGOS: "eye salve"
- PNEUMA: "ointment"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "eye salve" following LOGOS. KOLLOURION is specifically an eye medicament.

**Verse 19 — "reprove/rebuke/correct":**
- EMET: "reprove"
- LOGOS: "rebuke"
- PNEUMA: "correct"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "rebuke" following LOGOS.

**Verse 19 — "Be zealous/Be earnest":**
- EMET: "Be zealous"
- LOGOS: "Be zealous"
- PNEUMA: "Be earnest"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "Be zealous" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 20 — "I have stood" vs. "I stand":**
- EMET: "I have stood" (perfect tense)
- LOGOS: "I stand"
- PNEUMA: "I stand"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "I stand" (2-1 majority). The English present captures the stative sense of the Greek perfect.

**Verse 20 — "dine/share a meal":**
- EMET: "dine"
- LOGOS: "dine"
- PNEUMA: "share a meal"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "dine" (2-1 majority).

**Verse 21 — "give to him to sit" vs. "grant to him to sit" vs. "give the right to sit":**
- EMET: "give to him to sit"
- LOGOS: "grant to him to sit"
- PNEUMA: "give the right to sit"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted LOGOS. PNEUMA's "the right" is an interpretive addition.

**Verse 21 — "overcame and sat" vs. "conquered and sat down":**
- EMET: "overcame and sat"
- LOGOS: "conquered and sat down"
- PNEUMA: "overcame and sat down"
- SOPHIA's resolution: Adopted "conquered and sat down" following LOGOS, consistent with "conquers" throughout.

— SOPHIA
